parentQuestion: Feeling a gentle caress on your arm would likely involve all of the following EXCEPT ___.

scaffolds.q: Different receptors in the skin detect different types of touch.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The skin contains multiple types of mechanoreceptors - some for light touch, others for deep pressure, others for vibration.

scaffolds.q: Meissner's corpuscles detect light touch and are located in SUPERFICIAL skin layers.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Meissner's corpuscles are near the skin surface and are very sensitive to light touch and texture. A gentle caress would activate them.

scaffolds.q: Tactile (Merkel) discs detect light, sustained touch and are in superficial skin.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Merkel discs are in the basal epidermis and respond to sustained light touch. A hand resting on your arm would activate them.

scaffolds.q: Hair follicle receptors detect movement of body hairs.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Free nerve endings wrap around hair follicles. Even if a caress doesn't touch skin directly, bending the hairs triggers these receptors.

scaffolds.q: Lamellar (Pacinian) corpuscles are located DEEP in the skin.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Pacinian corpuscles are in the deep dermis and subcutaneous tissue. They're positioned to detect deep pressure and vibration, not gentle surface touch.

scaffolds.q: Pacinian corpuscles detect deep pressure and vibration, not light touch.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Pacinian corpuscles require significant pressure to deform them. A gentle caress wouldn't reach deep enough to activate them.

scaffolds.q: A gentle caress activates SUPERFICIAL receptors (Meissner's, Merkel, hair follicles) but not DEEP receptors (Pacinian).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Depth matters. Light touch affects the surface. Pacinian corpuscles are too deep to be stimulated by a gentle caress.

scaffolds.q: The location of a receptor (superficial vs deep) determines what kind of touch it responds to.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Receptor depth = stimulus type. Superficial receptors catch light touch. Deep receptors require more pressure. Architecture mirrors function.

scaffolds.q: A gentle caress would NOT activate lamellar (Pacinian) corpuscles because they are too deep.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! This is the answer. Pacinian corpuscles are in deep tissue. A gentle caress doesn't penetrate deep enough. They detect vibration and deep pressure, not surface caresses.
